mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units starts above 900,000 signals a robust demand for housing, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with HOUST > 900,000 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 109 signals a supply-side shock, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with INDPRO < 109 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with PAYEMS < 137,000 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real disposable personal income above 1.5% signals a robust demand for consumer goods, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with RSAFS > 1.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 6.5% signals a weakening labor market, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with UNRATE > 6.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a robust demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with GDPC1 > 3% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
